note: Recovered from workflow agent transcripts on 2026-05-29 (working-copy synthesis was clobbered with placeholder stubs during debugging). Full per-theme payloads live alongside in architect-verdicts/.

themes.verdict: Worth doing. All three claims (two ts-fx duplicate pairs + node.ts hand-rolling) are real and verified against source, and they share one root cause: there is no single "build envelope -> send -> correlate id -> decode" primitive, so the logic is re-implemented in four places (sendMessage/sendMessageAsync in bridge-protocol, the call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance family in pyodide-io, sendWarmupRequest/executeWorkerCall in node, and partially in each transport's send). The id-correlation gap (A3) is a direct consequence: because no layer owns correlation, HttpIO and PyodideIO simply skip it while ProcessIO duplicates its own. Consolidating buys real safety (correlation enforced once) and removes ~250 lines. Scope note: I'd fold the optimized-node.ts and protocol.ts deletions in as cheap co-located cleanup, but keep the BoundedContext/RuntimeExecution split (B7) as a SEPARATE follow-up theme — it touches the class hierarchy of every transport and is not required to land the dedup. Do not over-reach into B7 here.
dependsOn: delete-dead-code (bridge-core.ts / protocol.ts removal — protocol.ts re-home is cleaner once bridge-core.ts is already gone; if dead-code theme lands first, this theme only repoints one live import)

trim-barrel (B4 — settling whether SafeCodec.decodeResponse* and TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION are public determines whether their signature/name changes are breaking; ideally trim the barrel before changing these signatures)

themes.verdict: Worth doing, but with a SHARPLY narrowed target list. After reading source and tracing dynamic reachability, only 4 of the digest's top hotspots live on a path that real code executes: codec.ts:356 decodeEnvelopeCore (62/106), annotation-parser.ts:280 parse (58/75) + its helper splitTopLevel (17/36), mapper.ts:478 mapPresetType (41/66), and config/index.ts:120 validateConfig (26/39) + :259 loadConfigFile (19/33). The two highest-ranked entries after the codec function — analyzer.ts extractImports (27/92) and extractParameters (23/85) — live in src/core/analyzer.ts, which is DEAD (only non-test importer is the dead parallel-processor.ts). validation.ts:193 validateTypeHints (the medium-tier hotspot) is also dead by the same proof. Decomposing dead code is wasted effort and conflicts with the delete-dead-code theme, so I am explicitly excluding analyzer.ts and validation.ts. tywrap.ts:123 generate (38/91) is genuinely complex live code worth splitting; fetchPythonIr (25/27) is borderline and I scope it as optional. The codec, annotation-parser, and mapper functions are the genuine, high-leverage, behavior-preservable wins: each is a flat dispatch over a tagged discriminant (envelope marker / typing construct / preset name), which converts cleanly to a dispatch table with near-zero behavioral risk.

DO IT — with one correction to the review's file list. Five of the six candidates are genuinely dead by dynamic reachability and should be deleted outright: src/utils/parallel-processor.ts, src/utils/bundle-optimizer.ts, src/utils/memory-profiler.ts, src/runtime/bridge-core.ts, and src/runtime/optimized-node.ts. The sixth, src/runtime/protocol.ts, is NOT fully dead: its TYWRAP_PROTOCOL string const is dead (only bridge-core uses it), but its TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION numeric const has LIVE consumers (src/runtime/bridge-protocol.ts at lines 92/94/163 and test/runtime_node.test.ts:221). So protocol.ts must be drained-and-relocated, not blindly deleted: move TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION into transport.ts next to PROTOCOL_ID, drop TYWRAP_PROTOCOL entirely (it duplicates PROTOCOL_ID='tywrap/1'), then delete protocol.ts. Net ~2,400 lines of src plus ~1,300 lines of tests removed, the only utils->core layering inversion eliminated, and the protocol constants consolidated to one home. This is a clean, high-leverage subtraction with zero public-API loss because index.ts never exported any of these symbols.

Worth doing, scoped to the emit-pipeline extraction plus a minimal file split, not a full rewrite. The triplication is real and confirmed: the call-prelude, guard, and varargs logic is copy-pasted across three sites that differ ONLY in indentation, the error-label literal, and the final RPC call expression, about 210 lines whose only meaningful difference is three string parameters. Centralizing into a descriptor-driven emitter kills all 6 as-any casts in one helper, single-sources the keyword and positional-trim logic, and shrinks the file by about a third. Risk: this is string-template codegen with subtle whitespace contracts, so the refactor MUST be gated by a full-output characterization snapshot taken before any structural change; existing tests assert substrings, not full output, and will not catch a whitespace regression. REVIEW.md labels B3 large; the actual delta is medium because the three copies are mechanically identical, not semantically divergent. Do not chase the file split for its own sake; one new module emit-call.ts falls out naturally. Defer the ts-decl and runtime-shim split.

Worth doing, scoped. The expert's core claim is real and verified: 62 silent-skip `return`s (52 in runtime_node.test.ts, 10 in runtime_codec_scientific.test.ts) plus 7 `expect(true).toBe(true)` asserts make Python-dependent tests pass vacuously on any machine without Python. This is the single most misleading thing in the test suite. The fix (skipIf resolved once in beforeAll) is mechanical and low-risk for the silent-skips, medium for the new real coverage. Two of the four ts-fx duplicate pairs are tests of dead code (ParallelProcessor) and should be DELETED with the module, not deduped — confirmed via grep that ParallelProcessor has no real production caller (only a no-op setDebug at tywrap.ts:48). The Pyodide pair is a genuine byte-identical duplicate. The Path Utilities pair is a near-false-positive: token-identical bodies but exercising different runtimes (Bun-native vs tywrap pathUtils), so it is cross-runtime redundancy, not a deletable dupe. I recommend doing E1/E4 now (truth-telling + dedup + dead-test deletion), and treating E2/A1/A3 new coverage as a follow-on that depends on the source fixes landing first.

themes.theme: Restructure the Python IR analyzer (tywrap_ir) — capture dropped member categories, fix IRCache, single-source version, add pytest IR/protocol suites, and retire the redundant TS-side analyzer
themes.verdict: Worth doing, with one important refinement to the expert review's framing. The core defect is real and I confirmed it empirically: a type-safe wrapper generator that silently omits @property is incomplete by its own definition (F1/F2). But the review over-states the @classmethod/@staticmethod part — those members ARE captured (they pass inspect.ismethoddescriptor on 3.10+), they are just MISLABELED as ordinary methods and lose their cls/self semantics and any kind marker. The truly dropped categories are @property and @overload alternatives. I recommend: (1) do the member-category capture (high value, medium effort) — this is the load-bearing fix; (2) do the IRCache None fix, version single-sourcing, and pytest suites (all small/cheap, high trust value); (3) RETIRE src/core/analyzer.ts (PyAnalyzer) entirely as part of the parallel-processor deletion — I confirmed it is reachable in production ONLY through the dead parallel-processor subsystem, and the real generate() path goes through fetchPythonIr -> python -m tywrap_ir. This removes the two highest-complexity TS functions (extractImports 27/92, extractParameters 23/85) at zero production cost and eliminates a divergent second source of truth. I would SCOPE DOWN the ir.py module split: the file is 716 lines and cohesive (one extractor with helpers); a split is cosmetic and not worth the churn/import-path breakage unless the accessor work pushes it materially larger. Treat the split as optional.

Worth doing, with one scope cut. The complexity ts-fx flags is real but moderate (validateConfig 26/39, loadConfigFile 19/33) — these are the lowest-cog hotspots in the actionable-high list, so this theme's primary value is correctness/contract cleanup (G3/G4/G6 + VERSION drift), not raw complexity reduction. Decomposing validateConfig into schema-driven per-section validators is a clean, safe win that ALSO closes the http/pyodide/per-module validation gap (G6) in one move. Making per-module `runtime` optional/advisory is correct and low-risk (it is genuinely dead input — confirmed never read by generate()). Single-sourcing VERSION and unifying timeout naming are trivial and high-credibility. The loadConfigFile decomposition is worthwhile to extract the ext-dispatch into a table, but the .cts/.ts transpile branch is intrinsically complex and verified-correct — I recommend extracting it into a named helper untouched rather than rewriting it. SCOPE CUT: do not attempt to make `runtime` drive bridge selection (no AutoBridge exists); just demote the field.

Worth doing, high-confidence YES — this is the single cheapest, highest-leverage v1.0 move and it is a hard prerequisite for delete-dead-code. The evidence is unusually clean: tests reach every plumbing class via deep `../src/runtime/*.js` paths (never via the barrel), and the only documented/exampled adopter surface is exactly NodeBridge/PyodideBridge/HttpBridge + setRuntimeBridge + the arrow codec registration helpers + defineConfig/generate + the error classes + config/type exports. ProcessIO, HttpIO, PyodideIO, WorkerPool, PooledTransport, BridgeProtocol, BoundedContext, SafeCodec, Transport guards and ~15 validators appear in ZERO docs and have ZERO bare-`'tywrap'` consumers — they are pure semver liability. I scope DOWN from the review on one point: rather than create a new `tywrap/internal` subpath (which just re-freezes the same plumbing under a different name and invites the same masking), the plumbing should simply NOT be exported from any published entrypoint. The deep `src/runtime/*.js` paths tests use are not part of `package.json#exports`, so they are already non-public; keeping the classes file-level `export`ed for tests is fine and does not constitute a published contract. The ONLY thing worth a deliberate escape hatch is `SafeCodec`/`CodecOptions` (advanced users wiring a custom bridge) and the Transport interface contract — park those behind `./runtime` rather than the root, not `./internal`.

falsePositiveLedger: trim-public-api B4 tywrap/internal REFUTED: re-freezes modules into exports, re-creates masking; deep src/runtime test paths already non-public.
trim-public-api plumbing has no hidden barrel consumers: all src imports relative ./runtime; index.ts is a leaf.
delete-dead-code B1 parallel-processor 100ms pump PARTIALLY WRONG: setInterval in never-called init, unref-ed.
delete-dead-code memory-profiler:491 ESM crash UNDERSTATED: require in try/catch, no-ops; proof is zero importers.
delete-dead-code flat-delete protocol.ts WRONG: breaks live TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION (bridge-protocol.ts:24/92/94/163); relocate to transport.ts first.
decompose-hotspots analyzer.ts extractImports/extractParameters REFUTED: dead, delete not decompose.
decompose-hotspots validation.ts validateTypeHints REFUTED: zero src importers, dead.
decompose-hotspots splitTopLevel PARTIALLY REFUTED: irreducible state machine, do not dispatch-table.
generator-codegen B3 LARGE DOWNGRADED to medium: three copies mechanically identical.
generator-codegen six as-any casts are in emitted strings, not a generator.ts type hole.
generator-codegen generator.ts top-complexity god-file REFUTED: pain is duplication + 1291 lines.
python-ir F1 property/classmethod dropped OVER-STATED: classmethod/staticmethod captured but MISLABELED; only property/overload dropped.
python-ir IRCache None-bug as DATA LOSS REFUTED: cache-effectiveness bug; optimized_ir.py not on generate path.
python-ir ts-fx deadcode zero findings ignored: barrel masks it.
python-ir split ir.py REFUTED: 716 lines cohesive, breaks optimized_ir.py imports.
config-loading createRequire flags at 278/326 FALSE POSITIVES: createRequire(import.meta.url) is valid ESM.
config-loading G3 runtime field required is type-level only, never read at runtime.
config-loading G4 timeout/timeoutMs mismatch REFUTED: already bridged at tywrap.ts:172.
test-architecture Path Utilities pair NEAR-FALSE-POSITIVE: Bun-native vs tywrap pathUtils; extract fixture.
test-architecture delete optimized-node.test.ts PARTLY WRONG: tests live NodeBridge, keep.
test-architecture 63 silent-skips MISCOUNT: verified 62.
runtime-transport pyodide-io 2-method dup REFUTED: 4-method family, collapse all four.
runtime-transport warmup+executeWorkerCall as peers REFUTED: executeWorkerCall is the general base.
runtime-transport A3 ProcessIO same-layer correlation REFUTED: own it in BridgeProtocol/SafeCodec once.

releaseSequence.rationale: trim-public-api first un-blinds the dead-code scan (deep src/runtime test paths already non-public, zero test edits, no exports-map key change). delete-dead-code follows, now statically provable; protocol.ts drained-and-relocated (TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION to transport.ts as PROTOCOL_VERSION) not flat-deleted. test-architecture converts 62 silent-skips to skipIf and deletes dead-test files in lockstep. GATE: ts-fx re-scan confirms six masked dead modules and the utils-to-core edge and the ParallelProcessor/Pyodide dup pairs are gone; check:all green with no test edits beyond converted suites; test without Python proves vacuous tests now skip; living-app smoke green.

One breaking release behind a single migration guide. Order: decompose-hotspots + generator-codegen first (internal refactors snapshot-gated, both SKIP dead analyzer.ts/validation.ts functions); then runtime-transport (BridgeProtocol.dispatch + SafeCodec-owned id-correlation + node warmup unification; id-correlation enforcement is the load-bearing breaking change); then config-loading (schema validators covering http/pyodide/per-module, ext-dispatch verbatim, demote runtime, single-source VERSION, timeout JSDoc); python-ir LAST (IR schema +fields, IR_VERSION 0.2.0 to 0.3.0, regenerated wrappers, classmethod call-shape change) so its goldens absorb all output changes in one re-bless; test-architecture A1/A3 RED then GREEN after runtime-transport. GATE: check:all + python core/data + living-app smoke green; complexity re-scan confirms hotspots dropped; migration guide published.

themes: api-surface characterization test frozen as public contract
releaseSequence.rationale: No new churn. Promote the api-surface test to the canonical frozen contract (import-star from tywrap and tywrap/runtime asserting the exact key set). Semver boundary, locking a surface already validated by living-app smoke and a full migration cycle.

sequencing.executiveSummary:
tywrap is pre-1.0, ~0 users; breaking refactors are cheap. Critical path is static reachability: trim-public-api first (curating src/index.ts un-blinds the dead-code scan), then delete-dead-code (now provable; ~2400 src + ~1300 test lines incl analyzer.ts/validation.ts), then the rest. Strategy: one big-bang breaking v0.5.0 behind a single migration guide, fronted by incremental non-breaking v0.5.0-prep (barrel trim + dead-code + test truth-telling, gated by fx re-scan proving dead modules surfaced); v1.0.0 freezes the api-surface contract. Structural refactors go incremental behind characterization snapshots; deletions big-bang via typecheck+build. Verified: barrel index.ts:13-63, protocol.ts live consumer bridge-protocol.ts:24/92/94/163, analyzer only imported by parallel-processor, validation zero src importers, PROTOCOL_ID in transport.ts:20, VERSION 0.3.0 vs 0.4.0, TYWRAP_IR_VERSION 0.2.0 at tywrap.ts:25.</executiveSummary>
<parameter name="planMarkdown">tywrap v1.0 Refactoring Plan.

Section 1 Executive Summary and Strategy. Critical path is static reachability: trim-public-api then delete-dead-code then everything else. The over-broad src index barrel re-exports plumbing and dead modules, blinding the dead-code scan; curating it is the hard prerequisite that makes deletion provable. Strategy is incremental non-breaking v0.5.0-prep doing barrel trim and dead-code and test truth-telling, then one big-bang breaking v0.5.0 behind a single migration guide, then v1.0.0 API lock. Deletions are big-bang proven by typecheck and build. Structural refactors are incremental behind characterization snapshots. Behavior-changing themes bundle into v0.5.0. Gates are an fx re-scan proving dead modules surfaced from prep to v0.5.0, then check all plus Python suites plus living-app smoke plus complexity re-scan at v0.5.0, then api-surface lock at v1.0.0.

Section 2 False-Positive Ledger. See the structured falsePositiveLedger field for all 24 entries. Highlights. createRequire of import meta url at config index lines 278 and 326 is valid ESM and must not be fixed. analyzer and validation hotspots are dead so delete not decompose. generator pain is duplication and 1291 lines not complexity. The Path Utilities dup pair exercises Bun-native versus tywrap pathUtils so extract a fixture. The deadcode scan was empty because the barrel masks it. Expert refutes. tywrap internal re-freezes masking. Flat-delete protocol breaks the live version constant. The parallel-processor pump is in never-called init. classmethod and staticmethod are captured but mislabeled so only property and overload are dropped. The IRCache None-bug is cache-effectiveness not data loss. The runtime field is type-level only. timeout is already bridged. optimized-node test exercises the live NodeBridge.

Section 3 Per-Theme Summary as theme verdict risk effort deps. trim-public-api DO IT low small none. delete-dead-code DO IT low small dep trim-public-api. decompose-hotspots DO IT live only low medium dep delete-dead-code. generator-codegen DO IT medium medium snapshot-gated. runtime-transport DO IT medium medium deps delete-dead-code and trim-public-api. python-ir DO IT medium large dep delete-dead-code. config-loading DO IT no AutoBridge medium medium dep decompose overlap. test-architecture DO IT medium large deps delete-dead-code and runtime-transport.

Section 4 Release Sequence with Gates. See the structured releaseSequence field. Phase 0 v0.5.0-prep does trim-public-api then delete-dead-code then test truth-telling with a gate of fx re-scan plus check all plus a without-Python skip proof plus living-app smoke. Phase 1 v0.5.0 does decompose-hotspots and generator-codegen first, then runtime-transport, then config-loading, then python-ir last, then test-architecture A1 and A3, with a gate of check all plus python suites plus living-app smoke plus complexity re-scan plus migration guide. Phase 2 v1.0.0 does the api-surface lock with no churn.

Section 5 Consolidated Breaking Changes for a single migration guide. A Public surface. Remove from the root the plumbing ProcessIO HttpIO PyodideIO WorkerPool PooledTransport BridgeProtocol BoundedContext ValidationError and the roughly 16 validators. Delete the RuntimeBridge deprecated re-export. Move SafeCodec and CodecOptions and the Transport contract and PROTOCOL_ID to the tywrap runtime entrypoint. No exports-map key change. B Modules. Delete parallel-processor bundle-optimizer memory-profiler bridge-core optimized-node. Delete protocol with the version constant moved to transport as PROTOCOL_VERSION. Remove the OptimizedNodeBridge alias and use the tywrap node NodeBridge. Remove PyAnalyzer and analyzer and validation. Tree-sitter deps are droppable if unused. C Runtime. Response id mismatch now throws BridgeProtocolError on all bridges. SafeCodec decode gains an expectedId argument. Node warmup wording changes and full validation applies. D Config. The per-module runtime field becomes optional or removed and the CLI stops emitting it. Stricter validation rejects bad http baseURL and invalid typeHints and non-string arrays. VERSION is sourced from package json. auto stays a no-op alias. E Output. The IR schema gains method_kind is_abstract overloads and accessors. Wrappers emit accessors and static members and real overloads. classmethod call-shape changes. Overloads require Python 3.11 or newer. F Tests and CI. Deleted test files. Vacuous tests now skip. Ensure Python in CI.

Section 6 Detailed Per-Theme Plans.
6.1 trim-public-api. Curate the root to about 20 symbols, move SafeCodec and Transport to the runtime entrypoint not an internal one, keep file-level exports for deep-path tests. Steps add an api-surface test, delete plumbing and validator exports from the index file lines 13-17 18-19 36-42 45-63 and 68, remove the SafeCodec and Transport block lines 21 and 22-34, add re-exports to runtime index, update the api-surface test, run build typecheck test test-types check-node, run living-app smoke, run the fx deadcode re-scan, write docs migration.
6.2 delete-dead-code. Delete 5 modules and drain-and-relocate protocol while keeping cache and logger. Steps add PROTOCOL_VERSION to transport line 20, repoint bridge-protocol lines 24 92 94 163, update runtime node test lines 14 and 221, delete protocol, remove the tywrap factory import and call at lines 21 and 48 leaving line 47, delete the 5 modules, delete dead test files while keeping the bridge fixtures test, update docs README node and llms, run typecheck build test check-all, run the fx complexity and layering re-scan.
6.3 decompose-hotspots. Live functions only, exclude analyzer and validation. Targets are codec line 356 decodeEnvelopeCore into a dispatch table preserving per-marker error strings, annotation-parser line 280 parse into an ordered rule list leaving splitTopLevel intact, mapper line 478 mapPresetType into a data array, config line 120 validateConfig and line 259 loadConfigFile coordinated with config-loading, optionally the tywrap generate function. Steps green baseline, one function per commit, targeted re-run, check all, complexity diff.
6.4 generator-codegen. New emit-call module with a CallDescriptor and a CallSiteShape collapsing the triplication at lines 531-585 800-856 998-1052 and the guards at 496-525 859-890 1054-1085, with six as-any casts reduced to one location. Steps capture a full-output characterization snapshot on the unmodified generator first, extract helpers, rewrite the three sites byte-preserving, optionally a labeled commit changing the cast, run check all and a layout and duplicates scan.
6.5 runtime-transport. Own correlation in BridgeProtocol and SafeCodec not per-transport. A dispatch primitive builds id and protocol then encodes sends and decodes with an expectedId. sendMessage and sendMessageAsync at bridge-protocol lines 284 and 324 become thin wrappers. SafeCodec decodeResponseEnvelope asserts the id matches the expectedId or throws BridgeProtocolError. pyodide-io collapses all four methods at lines 376 403 430 457 into one dispatch. node collapses sendWarmupRequest line 566 into executeWorkerCall line 695 as the base. Steps characterize happy and error branches, add dispatch, repoint methods, add id-mismatch tests, update warmup assertions, run check all and the python core suite and a duplicates scan.
6.6 python-ir. Member-category capture in ir lines 481-495 mapping property to an accessor, unwrapping classmethod and staticmethod and tagging method_kind since they were captured but mislabeled, cached_property to readonly, abstractmethod to is_abstract. Overloads via a guarded typing get_overloads on 3.11 or newer. Single-source IR_VERSION at 0.3.0 and replace the TS version constant at tywrap line 25 with a CI drift check. Extend the PythonClass type and the transform mapClass at tywrap line 544 and feed overloads to the generator at lines 432-494. Retire analyzer. Add pytest IR and protocol suites. Add an IRCache MISS sentinel. Skip the ir file split. Steps fixtures and baseline snapshot, ir edits, IRCache fix, TS boundary, analyzer deletion, pytest suites, regenerate goldens and run check all and python suites and docs.
6.7 config-loading. A Field and assertField helper, split validateConfig into per-section validators covering http and pyodide and per-module, a loadConfigFile extension-dispatch table extracting the ts and cts branch verbatim since the createRequire flags are false positives, demote the per-module runtime field and stop the CLI emitting it at cli lines 53 and 75, single-source VERSION via a package json import, timeout option A keeping the name with a JSDoc, keep auto as a no-op alias. Steps characterization tests, a VERSION drift test and the import change, the schema refactor, extend validateRuntime and validatePythonModules, the loadConfigFile dispatch confirming createRequire byte-identical, demote the runtime field and update the CLI, run check all and a complexity scan, add a migration stub.
6.8 test-architecture. An env helper resolving capability flags once, convert 62 silent-skips to skipIf, delete 7 tautological asserts, delete dead-code test files in lockstep, dedupe the Pyodide pair, extract the Path-Utilities fixture without deleting either, fold the optimized-node test in, add new coverage for ProcessIO restart A1 and id-correlation A3 and a real-Pyodide round-trip E2 in a RED then GREEN flow. Steps env helper, convert suites, delete dead tests, dedupe, A1 and A3 and E2 RED then GREEN, baseline and with-Python and without-Python verification plus an fx re-scan plus a dead-test grep.
